name: 'copilot-agent-review-checklist'
description: 'Comprehensive checklist for reviewing GitHub Copilot coding agent PRs — covers quality, security, scope, tests, and governance'
agent: 'ask'
---

# Copilot Agent PR Review Checklist

Provide a structured review of a pull request generated by the GitHub Copilot coding agent. This goes beyond security to cover quality, correctness, scope, and maintainability.

## Review Categories

### 1. Scope & Intent (Did the agent do what was asked?)
- [ ] Changes align with the original task/issue description
- [ ] No unrelated files modified (scope creep)
- [ ] No "helpful" refactoring that wasn't requested
- [ ] Commit messages are meaningful and match the changes
- [ ] PR description accurately summarizes what was done

### 2. Code Quality
- [ ] Code follows project conventions (check relevant `.instructions.md` files)
- [ ] No dead code, commented-out blocks, or TODO placeholders left behind
- [ ] Variable/function names are clear and follow naming conventions
- [ ] No unnecessary type assertions (`as any`, `as unknown`)
- [ ] No overly complex logic where simpler alternatives exist
- [ ] Error handling is appropriate (not over-engineering, not ignoring)

### 3. Security (see also: `security-review-agent-output` prompt for deeper review)
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets or credentials
- [ ] No internal URLs or proprietary references in code/comments
- [ ] Input validation on new endpoints/handlers
- [ ] No new CORS relaxation without justification
- [ ] Dependencies are license-compatible and vulnerability-free

### 4. Testing
- [ ] New code has corresponding test coverage
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] Edge cases are covered (null, empty, invalid input)
- [ ] Test isolation — no shared state between tests (uses `reset*()` pattern)
- [ ] Tests follow the project's testing conventions (`testing.instructions.md`)

### 5. Documentation
- [ ] Swagger JSDoc added for new API endpoints
- [ ] README updated if new setup steps are needed
- [ ] Inline comments explain non-obvious logic (but aren't excessive)
- [ ] Type definitions are clear and documented where complex

### 6. Performance & Reliability
- [ ] No N+1 patterns or unbounded loops
- [ ] No memory leaks (event listeners, intervals without cleanup)
- [ ] Async operations have proper error handling
- [ ] No blocking operations in request handlers

### 7. Agent-Specific Gotchas
Common patterns to watch for in agent-generated code:

| Pattern | Risk | How to Spot |
|---------|------|-------------|
| Over-abstraction | Unnecessary complexity | Classes/factories for simple operations |
| Kitchen-sink imports | Bundle bloat | Large libraries for one function |
| Placeholder implementations | Incomplete features | `// TODO` or `throw new Error('Not implemented')` |
| Broad error catching | Silent failures | `catch (e) {}` or `catch (e) { console.log(e) }` |
| Copy-paste patterns | Maintenance burden | Repeated code blocks that should be shared |
| Hallucinated APIs | Runtime errors | Method calls to functions that don't exist |
| Wrong file locations | Project structure violations | New files in unexpected directories |
| Missing registrations | Dead code | Routes/components created but not mounted |

## Output Format

```markdown
## Agent PR Review

**Task**: {original task description}
**Branch**: {branch name}
**Files Changed**: {count}

### Overall Verdict: ✅ Approve / ⚠️ Request Changes / ❌ Reject

### Category Scores
| Category | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Scope & Intent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| Code Quality | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| Security | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| Testing | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| Documentation | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |
| Performance | ✅/⚠️/❌ | ... |

### Required Changes
1. ...

### Suggestions (non-blocking)
1. ...

### What the Agent Did Well
1. ...
```

## When to Use This Checklist

- Every time a Copilot coding agent creates a PR
- Before merging any automated code generation
- When reviewing code from any AI assistant (not just Copilot agent)
- As a teaching tool for teams new to AI-assisted development
